comments:
Summary:
The paper analyzes the sample complexity of estimating the optimal action-value function in finite discounted MDPs given access to a generative model. The main contributions are (i) an improved upper bound on the sample complexity of model-based Q-value iteration (QVI) of order O( N log(N/δ)/((1−γ)^3 ε^2) ) (with explicit constants), and (ii) a matching minimax lower bound showing that Θ( N log(N/δ)/((1−γ)^3 ε^2) ) transitions are necessary for any algorithm. The analysis leverages Bernstein concentration inequalities and a Bellman-like equation for the variance of the discounted return, allowing replacement of crude Vmax-based bounds and yielding the improved dependence on the horizon term 1/(1−γ). The paper contains detailed lemmas and a proof sketch of the lower bound via a hard class of MDPs.

Strengths:
- Addresses an important theoretical gap: closes the previously existing gap (1/(1−γ)^2 factor) between known upper and lower bounds for estimation with a generative model.
- Technique is natural and convincing: using variance-dependent concentration (Bernstein) together with a Bellman recursion for variance is an insightful idea and seems likely to be broadly useful.
- Provides both upper and matching lower bounds (minimax), which is a strong result in statistical learning theory for RL.
- Clear relation to prior work and potential implications for PAC-MDP bounds are discussed.

Weaknesses / Points for clarification:
- The paper omits some technical proofs due to space (promised in a long version). While standard in conference submissions, key lemmas are central to the new dependence on (1−γ); the authors should ensure the full proofs are available and checked carefully.
- There are a few typographical/repetition issues in the provided text (duplicated paragraphs and a stray expression that appears inconsistent; likely OCR or editing artifacts). The final submission should clean these up.
- The lower-bound construction imposes some parameter choices (e.g., p and a lower bound γ0 on γ in parts of the argument) and constraints like δ ≤ O(1/N) in statements — the scope of these constraints and their practical implications could be made more explicit. It would help readers to have a short paragraph summarizing regimes where the bound is tight and any corner cases.
- Constants are provided, but some constants (numerical factors) appear large; a brief discussion of whether these are artifacts of the analysis or reflect fundamental constants would be informative.

Recommendation:
This is a solid theoretical contribution that substantially advances understanding of sample complexity in the generative-model setting for RL by providing tight minimax rates and novel analytic techniques. I recommend acceptance.

Suggestions for authors:
- Include the omitted technical proofs in an appendix or an accompanying technical report and reference them in the main text.
- Fix duplicated/typo issues and ensure all parameter constraints (on γ, δ, ε) are clearly stated and discussed.
- If space permits, add a short section discussing implications for PAC-MDP/exploration settings and whether the variance-based analysis can be transplanted to yield improved exploration bounds (or obstacles to doing so).
